title: You Are a Badass at Making Money

chapters.title: Chart your own route to wealth
chapters.text:
People will volunteer opinions about what you should want, whether you asked or not. Without a clear inner compass, you drift toward their expectations and stay stuck for years — sometimes for life.

Everyone arrives with distinct desires, gifts, and aptitudes. Your task is to uncover them, nurture them, and let them shape the most honest version of you. That life costs money: experiences, tools, education, and freedom all have price tags. Financial abundance is therefore not vanity; it is fuel for self-expression.

Money is energy that helps you test your talents and pursue what matters.

Nobody is disqualified from prosperity, including you. Growth, joy, and identity work almost always require cash in a modern economy. The challenge is clarity — knowing what you want while noise competes for attention.

Listen inward. Follow what feels true, track what sparks happiness, and act on those signals instead of borrowed scripts. Focus on what is true and what matters to you by being intuitive, listening to your heart, and following through with what brings you happiness.

Beliefs live in the subconscious. At birth it is blank. Early on it absorbs family attitudes about money before conscious reasoning can filter them. Those impressions harden into "truth."

Thoughts stir emotions; emotions drive behavior; behavior builds reality. Feelings outmuscle logic, fear, and doubt when they run hot and positive. Strong, positive emotions will have no respect for fears or limitations.

Language reinforces belief. Your words back up your thoughts and your beliefs. When beliefs, thoughts, and words align, you control the financial story you tell — and the one you live.

You master your mindset when you get all three — your beliefs, your thoughts, and your words — to work together in a harmonious whole to create the financial reality you want.

This summary asks you to:

• reevaluate your mindset and tailor it to your actual needs
• discover the misconceptions you have about money and get rid of them
• make decisions and implement them

Jen Sincero uses wealth as a lever for becoming a bolder, truer self — not merely for stacking dollars. The most valuable part of this summary is beyond just making money.

chapters.title: Money carries energy
chapters.text:
Drift through life unchecked and your words, thoughts, and beliefs will steer the car while you sleep. Awareness is the antidote: notice what you say, think, and assume about cash until the pattern is obvious. We use money every day in exchange for goods or services, so your feelings about it need to be as clear as daylight.

Money is a medium of exchange — a measuring unit for giving and receiving. It is neither virtuous nor wicked on its own. It reports value; your choices give it character. Money is a messenger of value, always minding its business.

Money is only a tool. It will take you wherever you wish, but it will not replace you as the driver. ~ Ayn Rand

The same fifty dollars earned honestly vibrates differently from fifty dollars stolen on a subway. Align your inner frequency with the prosperity you seek by knowing the worth of what you offer and receiving payment with gratitude instead of embarrassment. This will happen when you are clear on the value of the product or service you are offering, and you are excited and grateful for receiving money for it instead of feeling weird and embarrassed.

Financial exchange is energetic trade between people.

Resources also flow from what Sincero calls Universal Intelligence — God, Spirit, or whatever name fits. It dwarfs you yet supports you. Thought is the dial that tunes you in. This makes your thoughts very important because this is how you connect with Universal Intelligence.

Focus on:

• your desire to have a specific quantity of money for a certain purpose
• your excitement to share something of value with others

These practices will help you obtain the money you want.

chapters.title: Break subconscious money blocks
chapters.text:
Hidden beliefs cap the amount of wealth you believe you deserve. Shifting from "I could never raise that much" to "there has to be a way" exposes the stories sabotaging you. Then you can question, dismantle, and replace them, acquiring the strength to grow.

If you treat money as dirty, you will unconsciously repel it.

Four practices help:

Step one

Face money fears directly. Figure out something scary and uncomfortable — a giant leap toward your goal of getting rich — and do it. For example, deposit for the rent for the office space you require for your spanking new business.

Step two

Watch your mouth. Say good things about your Universal Intelligence given the ability to make money. Those words will spring up positive thoughts and feelings. The thoughts are your superhighways to the spiritual world and Universal Intelligence.

Step three

Speak selectively. True, personal wisdom grows out of moments of silent contemplation. You can make more powerful choices, catch yourself in the moment, and ask yourself your motive for saying what you are about to say.

Step four

See yourself as having a friendly relationship with money.

Did you know? Invited to the Oprah Winfrey Show a while ago, Jim Carrey revealed that he used his mental mightiness to attract money ($10,000,000), fame, and success.

chapters.title: Want wealth for real reasons
chapters.text:
Prosperity requires passionate connection to why you want it. "Being rich" without purpose collapses the moment obstacles appear. Being clear on your purpose for seeking riches will help. Wishing to be rich on its own is not enough. There has to be meaning behind the money, or else you will slip into complacency when you encounter any difficulty.

Track the emotions tied to your financial goals; they determine whether you move or stall. Monitor the emotions surrounding your incentive for making money because they will prompt you to action.

Four steps keep desire sharp:

One

Be specific. Name the life money will fund. Currency has no personality — clarity about its job matters. You need to have a blazing desire to create a new financial reality.

Two

Stay aligned with your authentic self. Fighting your nature breeds stress, slows momentum, and produces blame. Listening inward unlocks flow, fresh ideas, and partnership with Universal Intelligence. Setbacks become lessons instead of verdicts. Inspired, brilliant ideas will come to you as you attain a flow state with Universal Intelligence.

Three

Reject false either-or choices. Culture peddles scarcity: love or money, vacation or car payment, goodness or wealth. Choose "and." Do meaningful work and get paid. Expand instead of shrinking. Our society thrives on fear. It wants you to limit yourself rather than stretch, love, or make money.

Four

Stay open to support from Universal Intelligence. Your part is to visualize, feel excited, and act. Then leave the rest for Universal Intelligence to work out on your behalf.

chapters.title: Rebuild your money mindset
chapters.text:
Thoughts sculpt financial reality, and you may choose them deliberately. Your thoughts create your financial reality, and you can choose of what you want to think about.

Five habits build a wealth mentality:

• Get clear on what you desire
• Hold that vision in your mind with a resolve to make it real
• Fall in love with your idea so that any self-limiting subconscious beliefs can't stop you
• Have faith that it's already yours
• Take purposeful action

Universal Intelligence treats focused thought as instruction. Universal Intelligence is listening to your thoughts, ready to take them as working orders and help you create the reality you envision.

To shift results, govern behavior and inner dialogue. Complaining forfeits progress — you can keep excuses or outcomes, not both. Don't complain. You can either have your excuses or your success. But not both.

Four additional moves accelerate change:

First, emphasize positives to raise frequency and spot opportunities, allies, and bigger possibilities. For example, the money-making opportunities, the people who can help you, and the ability to visualize a bigger life for yourself.

Second, intensify positive emotion so thoughts gain the power to rewrite beliefs and spark bold action. Your positive thoughts can access their superhero powers to create new expansive beliefs, fearless actions, and exciting new realities only through positive emotions.

Emotions are the kick that tells your body to materialize an idea. Your emotions are the motivating kicks in your butt — they alert your body to the fact that it's time to take action and make a thought a physical reality.

Third, use imagination to rehearse lives beyond default options. You will become a different person who creates whatever you set your mind on instead of settling for what you can get.

Fourth, commit with stubbornness. The universe responds to determination, yet you still carry the weight of follow-through. The outcomes you get depend directly on how determined you are.

Choosing wealth means confronting subconscious money stories you may have avoided for decades. Now, you are not only growing a new money-making mindset but also battling your limiting subconscious beliefs about money that you've probably not faced before.

chapters.title: Faith unlocks gratitude
chapters.text:
Faith is the part of you that bets on an unproven, magnificent future anyway. It launches you into territory your rational mind cannot map. Without it, the life you intend never gets built. Faith is the part of you that believes in the miracle of an unproven awesome reality that lies within your grasp. It is the rocket that you ride into uncharted territory in your wildest dreams.

Bold action is win-win: success rewards you; failure teaches you. Both beat hiding inside comfort. Taking a leap of faith is a win-win. You go big and take scary action steps to change your financial situation. If you nail it, good for you; if you don't, good for you too — you will learn lessons.

Faith is a muscle. The more you use it, the stronger it gets.

Gratitude tunes you to Universal Intelligence. Thankfulness matches the universe's generosity with your own, lifting frequency and inviting more gifts. Gratitude puts you in close contact with Universal Intelligence because by being filled with gratitude, you are tuning in your frequency to that of the Universe.

Gratitude gets you out of the loop of lowness, opens you to new possibilities, and sets you free. ~ Jen Sincero

Frustrated by income? Switch to gratitude anyway. Money is energy; appreciating what exists and what is arriving strengthens belief that more will follow. When you switch into gratitude mode and focus on feeling grateful for all you have and all that is coming your way, you strengthen your faith that the money will be there for you.

Serious wealth-building demands the stubbornness of someone who refuses backup plans. If plan B stays on the table, you have not decided. You need to decide that, for you, there is no plan B.

You cannot pursue everything at once. Split focus guarantees mediocre results. The truth is you can't do anything if you try to do everything.

Decisions feel scary because they close doors. Pick a path to riches and stay on it until it delivers; later you will have room for the extras you postponed. Eventually, you will find that you will get to do all the other things you couldn't do while you were busy sticking by your decision to get rich.

chapters.title: Choose high-frequency company
chapters.text:
Environment molds habits, style, and bank balance. The longer you stay immersed, the deeper the imprint. Your environment shapes everything about you — from your drinking habits to your dress and financial situation.

The same rule governs money. Friends and surroundings shape what feels normal, affordable, wise, or shameful. Income tends to mirror the earnings of the five people you see most. Your income level is the average of the incomes of the TOP-5 people you hang out with the most.

Without reflection, you accept your current setting as reality itself. Upgrading wealth therefore requires stepping into the world you want until it feels ordinary. Browse shops above your budget, walk neighborhoods you aim to buy into, test-drive the car you picture owning. It will jar you from your set ways of thinking, believing, and being. Also, it will allow the new environment to start becoming your new idea of normal.

Our environment shapes our perception of reality, so be conscious of the people you surround yourself with.

Who and what you surround yourself with has a telling effect on how you perceive money and what you feel about it. Your environment shapes your thoughts about what is cheap or not, what purchase is wise or not, and how much income you will allow yourself to make.

If your space drags you down, improve what you can:

• Put a fresh coat of paint on the walls
• Get the windows cleaned out
• Hang up pictures of your favorite places on earth
• Put some plants into your space

Let clothing, food, and music support joy. Keep company with people who cheer your growth and release those who drain it. High-frequency friends strengthen faith, sharpen skill, and celebrate your becoming. Plus, they will celebrate who you are.

chapters.title: More hustle is not the answer
chapters.text:
Scarcity breeds clingy relationships with money and buried beliefs that sabotage earning. Avoidance only prolongs the pain. Excavating guilt, missed chances, or shame around ambition is work worth doing. You tend to hold on to your unhealthy relationships with money, reluctant to peel off the layers of money-limiting beliefs. They lie dormant in your subconscious because you are afraid of what you will find beneath — maybe guilt for wanting to get rich or investment opportunities you blew.

Many people spend more energy perfecting selfies than designing a life they can fund. The average Jane spends more time in her life figuring out the perfect angle to take a selfie than she does figuring out what she really wants out of life, what it will cost her, and how she ought to increase her income to make it happen.

Wealth rarely comes from endless hours alone. It comes from choosing work that scales with your goals. To become rich, you don't need to dive into endless work hours but think first about what kind of work will make you wealthy.

Culture praises grinding, yet rich people seldom trade every hour for wages. Start with the number you need and reverse-engineer the behavior that produces it. Society teaches you to work harder because money will somehow come if you put in more work. That is not true.

Something must change — new business, uncomfortable job, bolder offer. Playing not to lose keeps you broke. Play to win. You cannot play it safe if you want to be rich. You need to play to win instead of playing not to lose.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Prosperity is simpler than most people admit. Even without inherited advantage, you can build wealth by rewiring how you think about money. Getting rich is very easy. There are only some things that you need to do to hit the goldmine of your life.

Begin with why you want it and how much "enough" means to you. Nobody needs everything. Next, identify what must change in your current earning strategy. Root out prejudice, shame, or magical thinking about cash. Perhaps, you have some dangerous misconceptions or prejudice against money or the kind of relationship you can have with it. Carefully examine your mind to detect and eliminate them.

Most people underestimate thought's effect on external results, which is why harder work alone fails. Many people tend to underestimate the influence our thoughts have on the external reality, and that is why no matter how hard they work, they still cannot achieve the level of wealth they desire.

Along the way, nurture a healthy relationship with money. Raise your frequency, attract aligned opportunities, and stay connected to Universal Intelligence — a force inclined toward generosity. Connect with Universal Intelligence. It is in the business of bestowing gifts on humankind.

Wealth can expand confidence and self-trust. Master your inner obstacles and money stops feeling like the main problem. You will be rich beyond your wildest imagination. A welcome side-effect of that can be a boosted level of self-confidence and trust in your strength. Once you manage to overcome yourself, money will no longer be an issue.

Try this


• Pick up your journal and describe in detail the authentic life you desire. What amount of money will get you there? What would you do or change about what you are doing presently to get you there?
• Is your wealth mindset in top shape? Do your beliefs, thoughts, and words align with the authentic life you dream about? If they don't, what are you doing about it?
• Write out three areas of your financial life where you opted for the "either-or" life. Now write out how you would transit from the "either-or" life to the "and" life in these areas.

Read it till the end?
